levels.body: Even though the context makes clear that Fern is from Dorset, there is no evidence that "nowt" was used for "nothing" in Dorset dialect. In his *Poems of Rural Life, in the Dorset Dialect*, William Barnes uses neither "nowt" nor "nawt". Bertil Widén only records / nɔ́þən / as "nothing" in Dorset dialect (cf. 71). Dickens here mistakenly uses a Yorkshire dialect word (cf. Gerson 104) for a speaker from Dorset.
worksCited: Barnes, William. *Poems of Rural Life, in the Dorset Dialect, With a Dissertation and Glossary*. London: John Russell Smith, 1844. Print.
Gerson, Stanley. *Sound and Symbol in the Dialogue of the Works of Charles Dickens*. Stockolm et al.: Almqvist & Wiksell, 1967. Print.
Widén, Bertil. *Studies on the Dorset Dialect*. Nendeln: Kraus, 1968. Print.
